2605.03799.title: Natural Language Processing: A Comprehensive Practical Guide from Tokenisation to RLHF
2605.03799.paper_summary: This preprint presents a systematic, research-oriented practicum that guides the reader through the entire modern NLP pipeline: from tokenisation and vectorisation to fine-tuning of large language models, retrieval-augmented generation, and reinforcement learning from human feedback. Twelve hands-on sessions combine concise theory with detailed implementation plans, formalised evaluation metrics, and transparent assessment criteria. The work is not a conventional textbook: it is designed as a reproducible research artefact where every session requires publishing code, models, and reports in public repositories. All experiments are conducted on a single evolving corpus, and the work advocates open-weight models over commercial APIs, with special attention to the Hugging Face ecosystem. The material is enriched by original research on low-resource languages, incorporating linguistic resources for Tajik and Tatar (subword tokenisers, embeddings, lexical databases, and transliteration benchmarks), demonstrating how modern NLP can be adapted to data-scarce environments. Designed for senior undergraduates, graduate students, and practising developers seeking to implement, compare, and deploy methods from classical ML to state-of-the-art LLM-based systems.
2605.03799.paper_summary_zh: 這份預印本提出了一個系統性的、以研究為導向的實習課程，引導讀者了解整個現代自然語言處理（NLP）流程：從分詞和向量化到大型語言模型的微調、檢索增強生成以及從人類反饋中進行強化學習。十二個實作課程結合了簡潔的理論與詳細的實施計劃、正式的評估指標和透明的評估標準。這項工作並不是一本傳統的教科書：它被設計為一個可重複的研究產物，每個課程都需要在公共存儲庫中發布代碼、模型和報告。所有實驗都在一個不斷演變的語料庫上進行，並且該工作提倡開放權重模型而非商業API，特別關注Hugging Face生態系統。這些材料通過對低資源語言的原創研究得到了豐富，納入了塔吉克語和塔塔爾語的語言資源（子詞分詞器、嵌入、詞彙數據庫和音譯基準），展示了現代NLP如何適應數據稀缺的環境。這項工作旨在為高年級本科生、研究生和尋求實施、比較和部署從經典機器學習到最先進的基於大型語言模型系統的方法的實踐開發者提供支持。
2605.03799.author: Mullosharaf K. Arabov et.al.
